agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and entity money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (sanctions screening), and fraud prevention (account locking). The product operates in a regulated financial services domain where KYC/AML compliance is non-negotiable.","bounded_context":"Wallet Transfers & Account Security","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that must be completed before a user can send money or withdraw funds."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to $5,000/day."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account with incomplete KYC that has passed some verification steps. Can transact up to $500/day but cannot send or withdraw (implied — spec only mentions daily limit, not send/withdraw permissions)."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account with no KYC completed. Can receive money only — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement (T+1)","definition":"The point at which a transaction becomes final and irreversible, occurring one business day after initiation."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A recipient (person or organization) appearing on a regulatory sanctions list. All transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Only a support agent can unlock the account."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum cumulative amount a user can transfer in a calendar day, determined by verification tier."},{"term":"High-Value Transaction","definition":"Any transaction exceeding $1,000, which triggers a mandatory notification to the user."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A user must complete full KYC verification before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement — allowing unverified users to send money violates AML/KYC law and exposes the platform to legal liability. This is not a policy a VP can waive.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to initiate a send from an account where KYC status is not 'verified' must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw funds.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory constraint — receiving funds into a custodial account is permissible, but outbound movement of funds without identity verification is a compliance violation.","testable_assertion":"An unverified account must accept incoming transfers and must reject all outbound transfer and withdrawal requests."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit for verified accounts is $5,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy threshold that could be adjusted based on risk appetite, market conditions, or product tier changes. The concept of a limit is invariant, but the amount is policy.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a verified account in a calendar day must not exceed $5,000."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit for partially-verified accounts is $500.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy threshold — the specific dollar amount is a tunable parameter, not a fundamental constraint.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a partially-verified account in a calendar day must not exceed $500."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Financial integrity constraint — reversing settled transactions corrupts ledger state and violates double-entry accounting invariants. Post-settlement corrections require compensating transactions, not reversals.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction with settlement_status = 'settled' must be rejected. Only transactions in 'pending' status (pre-T+1) may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal/regulatory mandate — processing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a federal crime (OFAC). This cannot be overridden by any business policy.","testable_assertion":"Every outbound transfer must be screened against the sanctions list before execution; any match must result in immediate blocking of the transaction with no funds movement."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Five consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity constraint — without this, the system is vulnerable to brute-force attacks on financial accounts. The lock mechanism itself is invariant; the threshold (5) is policy but the spec states it as a must.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts for the same account, the account status must change to 'locked' and all subsequent login attempts (even with correct credentials) must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only a support agent can unlock a locked account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity — self-service unlock would defeat the purpose of the lock by allowing an attacker who eventually guesses the password to proceed. Human-in-the-loop is required for fraud verification.","testable_assertion":"There must be no automated or self-service mechanism to transition an account from 'locked' to 'active'. Only an authenticated support agent action can perform this transition."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy for user awareness and fraud detection. The threshold and notification mechanism are tunable parameters, not system integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is created, a notification event must be emitted to the account holder before or at the time of transaction completion."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"they attempt to send money to another user","then":"the transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"another user sends them money","then":"the transfer succeeds and the funds are credited to the unverified account"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"they attempt to withdraw funds","then":"the withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified account that has already transferred $4,800 today","when":"the user attempts to send $300","then":"the transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A partially-verified account that has already transferred $400 today","when":"the user attempts to send $150","then":"the transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A transaction that was initiated yesterday and has now settled (T+1)","when":"the sender requests a reversal","then":"the reversal is rejected with a message that settled transactions cannot be reversed"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction that was initiated minutes ago and has not yet settled","when":"the sender requests a reversal","then":"the reversal is processed and funds are returned to the sender"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A user attempts to transfer funds to a recipient","when":"the recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"the transaction is blocked immediately, no funds are moved, and the event is logged for compliance"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user has failed login 4 times consecutively","when":"they fail a 5th consecutive login attempt","then":"the account is locked and a notification is sent to the account holder"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A locked account","when":"the user attempts to log in with correct credentials","then":"login is denied and the user is informed their account is locked and must contact support"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A locked account","when":"a support agent initiates an unlock","then":"the account is unlocked and the failed login counter is reset to zero"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A verified user initiating a transfer of $1,500","when":"the transaction is created","then":"a notification is sent to the user about the transaction"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A verified user initiating a transfer of $999","when":"the transaction is created","then":"no high-value transaction notification is triggered"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A user has failed login 3 times, then succeeds","when":"they subsequently fail 4 more times","then":"the account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A partially-verified account attempts to send money","expected_behavior":"Spec says unverified cannot send, and partially-verified has a $500 limit — implying partially-verified CAN send up to $500/day. This is the intended interpretation but should be confirmed, since BR-1 says 'complete KYC' is required to send."},{"scenario":"Transaction amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"Notification rule says 'over $1,000', so exactly $1,000 should NOT trigger a notification. The boundary is strictly greater than."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary — transfer that lands exactly at $5,000 cumulative","expected_behavior":"Should be allowed, as the limit is $5,000, not 'less than $5,000'. A subsequent transfer of any amount should be rejected."},{"scenario":"Sanctions list is updated after a transaction is initiated but before settlement","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether post-initiation sanctions screening is required. Best practice is to screen at both initiation and settlement."},{"scenario":"Account is locked during an in-flight transaction","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address whether pending transactions should be frozen or allowed to complete when an account is locked."},{"scenario":"User receives money that pushes their balance above limits while unverified","expected_behavior":"Receiving has no limit mentioned in the spec — unverified accounts can receive unlimited inbound funds. They simply cannot move those funds out."},{"scenario":"Multiple concurrent transfers that individually are under the daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce the daily limit atomically — race conditions in concurrent transfer processing could allow limit bypass if not handled with proper locking or serialization."},{"scenario":"Timezone for 'daily' limit reset","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define what timezone or clock defines a 'day' for daily transfer limits. This matters for users near midnight boundaries."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Direct revenue impact from transaction fees. Incorrect limit enforcement could lead to regulatory fines (KYC/AML violations carry penalties in the millions). Sanctions violations carry criminal liability. Overly restrictive limits or false-positive sanctions blocks reduce transaction volume and revenue.","operational":"Support team workload is directly impacted by account locking policy — every locked account requires manual intervention. Sanctions screening must integrate with real-time updated lists (OFAC SDN). Settlement timing (T+1) creates a window where funds are in-flight and reconciliation must be managed.","user":"Friction from KYC requirements may reduce conversion. Account locking after 5 attempts protects users but can frustrate legitimate users who forget passwords. Notifications for high-value transactions build trust and enable fraud detection. Inability to reverse settled transactions may cause dissatisfaction.","risk":"Primary risks are regulatory (KYC/AML/sanctions non-compliance), fraud (brute-force attacks, limit circumvention), and operational (support burden from locked accounts). The T+1 settlement window creates exposure to fraud where a sender's account is compromised after initiation but before settlement.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Senders)","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality. Subject to daily limits, account locking, and irreversible settlements."},{"group":"End Users (Recipients)","impact":"Can receive funds regardless of verification status, providing a smooth onboarding funnel."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Responsible for maintaining sanctions lists, KYC processes, and regulatory reporting. Must be notified of blocked sanctions transfers."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Sole authority to unlock accounts — direct workload impact from every account lock event."},{"group":"Finance/Treasury","impact":"Must manage T+1 settlement reconciliation, float management, and daily limit aggregate reporting."},{"group":"Product/Growth Team","impact":"KYC friction and daily limits affect conversion funnels and user activation metrics."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured and covers the major domains (verification tiers, limits, settlement, sanctions, security, notifications) with clear rules. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified: the partially-verified account's send permissions appear to contradict the KYC-required-to-send rule, timezone for daily limits is undefined, and post-initiation sanctions re-screening is not addressed. These are common gaps in product specs rather than contradictions.","high_confidence_areas":["KYC-gated send/withdraw permissions for unverified accounts","Account locking mechanism and unlock process","Settlement finality at T+1","Sanctions blocking requirement","Notification threshold for transactions over $1,000"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partially-verified accounts can send (up to $500) — the $500 limit implies yes, but BR-1 says full KYC is required to send, creating tension","Timezone definition for daily limit resets","Whether sanctions screening occurs only at initiation or also at settlement","Behavior of in-flight transactions when an account is locked","Whether the $1,000 notification threshold is strictly greater than or greater-than-or-equal"],"assumptions":["Partially-verified accounts CAN send money up to their $500 daily limit, interpreting the $500 limit as implying send capability despite the general KYC requirement","Daily limits apply to outbound transfers only, not inbound receives","The $1,000 notification threshold is strictly greater than (not >=)","A successful login resets the consecutive failed attempt counter to zero","T+1 means one business day, not one calendar day"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The product operates in a regulated financial services domain where KYC/AML compliance is non-negotiable.","bounded_context":"Wallet Transfers & Account Security","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process th

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this digital wallet domain specification carefully.

The spec is clear and well-defined. Let me classify business rules as invariants vs policies, write acceptance criteria, and assess confidence.

No contradictions or ambiguities that rise to escalation level. The spec is relatively complete for what it covers.
